perturbation.original_text: 1.2 In agreeing to perform the Work Plan, Cargill represents and warrants that: 1.2.1 Cargill has the capability, experience, and means necessary to perform the Work Plan, and the Work Plan will be performed using personnel, equipment, and material qualified and suitable to perform the Work Plan requested; th Cargill Confidential - 2 - * Confidential treatment requested 1.2.2 Cargill will provide properly trained and informed personnel, and Cargill will be solely responsible for the negligent acts, errors and omissions of its employees, subcontractors, and agents and for any other person performing services under this Development Agreement at the direct request of Cargill; 1.2.3 Cargill will perform the Work Plan in a workmanlike manner with reasonable skill and care ordinarily exercised by members of the profession practicing under similar conditions and in accordance with accepted industry practices and professional guidelines; 1.2.4 Subject to Section 13.1, Cargill has in effect and will maintain in effect all permits, licenses and other authorizations necessary to perform the Work Plan; and 1.2.5 No other party has rights to its services as described in the Work Plan, and that a work assignment from any third party shall not be accepted, or work by Cargill aloneshall not be conducted, to develop CB1 or other microorganisms that will be used to (a) directly produce succinic acid and salts thereof, or (b) indirectly produce succinic acid and salts thereof (for example, Cargill may develop microorganisms to produce precursors of succinic acid, such as fumaric acid and malic acid, so long as such precursors are not converted to succinic acid, such as by chemical modification), for the Term (as defined in Section 10.1) of this Development Agreement. For purposes of clarity, and as examples, Cargill may sell dextrose as fermentation feedstock to third parties, who may use such dextrose to produce succinic acid; also, Cargill may modify starches to make succinic acid starch derivatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 In agreeing to perform the Work Plan, Cargill represents and warrants that: 1.2.1 Cargill will make commercially reasonable efforts to perform the Work Plan, and the Work Plan will be performed using personnel and material deemed suitable at Cargill's discretion; th Cargill Confidential - 2 - * Confidential treatment requested 1.2.2 Cargill will endeavor to provide adequately trained personnel but is not responsible for any negligent acts, errors and omissions of its employees, subcontractors, and agents except in cases of gross negligence. 1.2.3 Cargill will attempt to perform the Work Plan in a workmanlike manner but provides no guarantees regarding skill or care. 1.2.4 Cargill makes no representation regarding permits, licenses and other authorizations necessary to perform the Work Plan; and 1.2.5 Other parties may have rights to its services as described in the Work Plan, and Cargill may accept work assignments from third parties to develop CB1 or other microorganisms that will be used to (a) directly produce succinic acid and salts thereof, or (b) indirectly produce succinic acid and salts thereof, at its sole discretion, notwithstanding the Term (as defined in Section 10.1) of this Development Agreement. For purposes of clarity, and as examples, Cargill may sell dextrose as fermentation feedstock to third parties, who may use such dextrose to produce succinic acid; also, Cargill may modify starches to make succinic acid starch derivatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the language from 'represents and warrants' to weaker terms like 'commercially reasonable efforts' and removing the guarantee of skill creates uncertainty. It directly contradicts the original intent of Cargill's obligations and level of accountability. By removing responsibility for negligent acts, it contradicts section 1.2.2 where the original document says that 'Cargill will be solely responsible for the negligent acts, errors and omissions of its employees, subcontractors, and agents'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 In agreeing to perform the Work Plan, Cargill represents and warrants that: 1.2.1 Cargill has the capability, experience, and means necessary to perform the Work Plan, and the Work Plan will be performed using personnel, equipment, and material qualified and suitable to perform the Work Plan requested; th Cargill Confidential - 2 - * Confidential treatment requested 1.2.2 Cargill will provide properly trained and informed personnel, and Cargill will be solely responsible for the negligent acts, errors and omissions of its employees, subcontractors, and agents and for any other person performing services under this Development Agreement at the direct request of Cargill; 1.2.3 Cargill will perform the Work Plan in a workmanlike manner with reasonable skill and care ordinarily exercised by members of the profession practicing under similar conditions and in accordance with accepted industry practices and professional guidelines; 1.2.4 Subject to Section 13.1, Cargill has in effect and will maintain in effect all permits, licenses and other authorizations necessary to perform the Work Plan; and 1.2.5 No other party has rights to its services as described in the Work Plan, and that a work assignment from any third party shall not be accepted, or work by Cargill aloneshall not be conducted, to develop CB1 or other microorganisms that will be used to (a) directly produce succinic acid and salts thereof, or (b) indirectly produce succinic acid and salts thereof (for example, Cargill may develop microorganisms to produce precursors of succinic acid, such as fumaric acid and malic acid, so long as such precursors are not converted to succinic acid, such as by chemical modification), for the Term (as defined in Section 10.1) of this Development Agreement. For purposes of clarity, and as examples, Cargill may sell dextrose as fermentation feedstock to third parties, who may use such dextrose to produce succinic acid; also, Cargill may modify starches to make succinic acid starch derivatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens Cargill's obligations and responsibilities, directly contradicting the original representations and warranties outlined in the contradicted text. For example, the original text states "Cargill will be solely responsible for the negligent acts, errors and omissions of its employees, subcontractors, and agents". The changed text states "Cargill will endeavor to provide adequately trained personnel but is not responsible for any negligent acts, errors and omissions of its employees, subcontractors, and agents except in cases of gross negligence." This shift from sole responsibility to responsibility only in cases of gross negligence creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Improvements. Any invention or discovery relating to the Work Plan, in whole or in part, that is conceived during the term of this Development Agreement shall be an "Improvement". The scope for Improvements will be limited to the CB1 strain itself and the [***] (the "Field"), but shall exclude [***]. Bioamber will own any Improvement in the field of succinic acid and salts thereof and such Improvement shall be designated "Bioamber Improvements". Cargill will own any Improvement in all fields other than the field of succinic acid and salts thereof, subject to the rights provided in Section 5.2.1 below, and such Improvement shall be designated "Cargill Improvements". In the event it is not clear as to ownership of any Improvement as described in this Section 5.2, in other words, if it is not clear whether an invention or discovery is either a Bioamber Improvement or a Cargill Improvement, such Improvement shall be [***] and such Improvement shall be designated "Joint Improvements". For purposes of clarity, as examples, Cargill Improvements are those inventions relating to fumaric acid and malic acid. Additionally, where an Improvement has applications both in the Field and outside the Field, then such Improvement, as it applies to the Field, shall be a Bioamber Improvement and, as it applies outside the Field, such Improvement shall be a Cargill Improvement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Improvements. Any invention or discovery relating to the Work Plan, in whole or in part, that is conceived during the term of this Development Agreement may, at Cargill’s sole discretion, be considered an "Improvement". The scope for Improvements will be limited to the CB1 strain itself and the [***] (the "Field"), but shall exclude [***]. Bioamber may own any Improvement in the field of succinic acid and salts thereof and such Improvement may be designated "Bioamber Improvements" at Cargill's sole discretion. Cargill will own any Improvement in all fields other than the field of succinic acid and salts thereof, subject to the rights provided in Section 5.2.1 below, and such Improvement shall be designated "Cargill Improvements". In the event it is not clear as to ownership of any Improvement as described in this Section 5.2, in other words, if it is not clear whether an invention or discovery is either a Bioamber Improvement or a Cargill Improvement, such Improvement shall be determined solely by Cargill and such Improvement shall be designated "Joint Improvements". For purposes of clarity, as examples, Cargill Improvements are those inventions relating to fumaric acid and malic acid. Additionally, where an Improvement has applications both in the Field and outside the Field, then such Improvement, as it applies to the Field, shall be designated based on Cargill's approval and, as it applies outside the Field, such Improvement shall be a Cargill Improvement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be an improvement' to 'may, at Cargill's sole discretion' introduces ambiguity about what constitutes an 'Improvement'. Similarly, giving Cargill the sole discretion to determine ownership contradicts the original intent of clearly defined ownership based on the Field. Specifically, this pertubation directly contradicts within section 5.2 where the document originally stated that 'Bioamber will own any Improvement in the field of succinic acid and salts thereof and such Improvement shall be designated "Bioamber Improvements"'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Improvements. Any invention or discovery relating to the Work Plan, in whole or in part, that is conceived during the term of this Development Agreement shall be an "Improvement". The scope for Improvements will be limited to the CB1 strain itself and the [***] (the "Field"), but shall exclude [***]. Bioamber will own any Improvement in the field of succinic acid and salts thereof and such Improvement shall be designated "Bioamber Improvements". Cargill will own any Improvement in all fields other than the field of succinic acid and salts thereof, subject to the rights provided in Section 5.2.1 below, and such Improvement shall be designated "Cargill Improvements". In the event it is not clear as to ownership of any Improvement as described in this Section 5.2, in other words, if it is not clear whether an invention or discovery is either a Bioamber Improvement or a Cargill Improvement, such Improvement shall be [***] and such Improvement shall be designated "Joint Improvements". For purposes of clarity, as examples, Cargill Improvements are those inventions relating to fumaric acid and malic acid. Additionally, where an Improvement has applications both in the Field and outside the Field, then such Improvement, as it applies to the Field, shall be a Bioamber Improvement and, as it applies outside the Field, such Improvement shall be a Cargill Improvement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by granting Cargill sole discretion over determining what constitutes an 'Improvement' and its ownership, directly conflicting with the original text (which is also the contradicted text) that states 'Bioamber will own any Improvement in the field of succinic acid and salts thereof and such Improvement shall be designated "Bioamber Improvements"'. The original text provides a clear and defined ownership structure, whereas the changed text gives Cargill the power to override this structure, thus creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding ownership, making the original text no longer operative.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 This Development Agreement will begin on the Effective Date and continue for four (4) years unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 10.2, or unless the parties extend the term by mutual written Development Agreement ("Term"). 10.2 Either Party may terminate this Development Agreement by giving written notice to the other Party, (a)in the event the other Party's bankruptcy, insolvency, or the filing of a petition therefore; and (b) the other Party materially defaults in the performance of its obligations hereunder. This Development Agreement shall also terminate upon mutual written agreement by the Parties. Further, in the event there is a dispute as to whether or not Cargill has missed a certain Milestone, then the Target Date for that Milestone shall be tolled until the Parties, acting in good faith, have settled such dispute in writing between themselves or through an independent expert.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 This Development Agreement will begin on the Effective Date and continue indefinitely, unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 10.2, or unless Cargill decides to terminate it with no reason given("Term"). 10.2 Either Party may terminate this Development Agreement by giving written notice to the other Party, (a)in the event the other Party's bankruptcy, insolvency, or the filing of a petition therefore; and (b) the other Party materially defaults in the performance of its obligations hereunder. This Development Agreement shall also terminate upon mutual written agreement by the Parties, unless Cargill disagrees with it. Further, in the event there is a dispute as to whether or not Cargill has missed a certain Milestone, then Cargill will determine in its sole discretion whether or not the Target Date for that Milestone shall be tolled until the Parties, acting in good faith, have settled such dispute in writing between themselves or through an independent expert.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term from a fixed four years to 'indefinitely' and adding that Cargill can terminate for no reason creates a contradiction. In the original document, section 10.1 stated that the 'Development Agreement will begin on the Effective Date and continue for four (4) years'. The updated text gives conflicting information about the term's duration, undermining Bioamber's expectations and potentially affecting their planning, and also, by adding a unilateral desition by Cargill, will make Cargill biased on its desicion-making.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 This Development Agreement will begin on the Effective Date and continue for four (4) years unless earlier terminated pursuant to Section 10.2, or unless the parties extend the term by mutual written Development Agreement ("Term"). 10.2 Either Party may terminate this Development Agreement by giving written notice to the other Party, (a)in the event the other Party's bankruptcy, insolvency, or the filing of a petition therefore; and (b) the other Party materially defaults in the performance of its obligations hereunder. This Development Agreement shall also terminate upon mutual written agreement by the Parties. Further, in the event there is a dispute as to whether or not Cargill has missed a certain Milestone, then the Target Date for that Milestone shall be tolled until the Parties, acting in good faith, have settled such dispute in writing between themselves or through an independent expert.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. Originally, section 10.1 states a fixed term of four years. The changed text in 10.1 states the agreement continues 'indefinitely' but can be terminated by Cargill at any time, with no reason given. This directly contradicts the initial four-year term and the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated as outlined in both the original section 10.1 and 10.2, undermining the other party's expectations and creating an enforceable uncertainty, because the termination conditions in section 10.2 become irrelevant. Additionally, granting Cargill sole discretion in milestone disputes contradicts the original good faith requirement.
